scope.summary: Commits the C2 wire-contract fixture: hand-written literal MCP initialize frames carrying the stdio init token at params._meta["oraclemcp/initToken"], driven through the real serve_stdio_with_io loop, pinning Mismatch-vs-Missing and the three ways a client silently lands on Missing, with the discoverability half committed red for bead oraclemcp-091-b3-stdio-token-nzmiv.
out_of_scope: Documenting the init-token path, and naming it in the Missing error text — B3 (oraclemcp-091-b3-stdio-token-nzmiv) owns both; this bead owns only the fixture.
The S13 half of B3 (whether mainstream MCP clients can inject params._meta at all, and whether the secure_stdio snippet should keep advertising the flow); C9 covers the snippet.
Any change to StdioAuthPolicy, the extraction path, or the constant-time comparison.

entry_points.name: stdio transport initialize handshake under StdioAuthPolicy::Required
entry_points.trace: c2_literal_frame_authenticates_against_the_matching_token feeds a committed literal frame through serve_stdio_with_io and asserts a JSON-RPC result rather than an error; c2_literal_frame_is_a_mismatch_not_a_miss_against_a_different_token asserts the same frame against a different expected token reports mismatch, proving the literal path still resolves

entry_points.name: init-token extraction from params._meta on initialize
entry_points.trace: c2_absent_and_misplaced_tokens_all_read_as_missing drives five frames (no _meta, three plausibly guessed keys, a non-string value at the right path) and asserts every one reports the token as missing

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test c2_stdio_init_token_literal_frame (3 passed, 2 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run of those two. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.

The two discoverability assertions are committed red by design and do not execute in the default run; they are not cited as proof of anything here. Their recorded red output is the deliverable, and B3 flips them green by deleting the #[ignore]s with the assertions unchanged.

The documentation assertion scans README.md, oraclemcp.example.toml, robot_docs.rs, and docs/**/*.md excluding docs/plan/. It proves the string is present somewhere an integrator would look; it cannot prove the surrounding prose is correct or usable.
This fixture proves the server accepts a correctly shaped frame. It says nothing about whether any real MCP client can be configured to emit params._meta — that is the open S13 question inside B3.
follow_ups: oraclemcp-091-b3-stdio-token-nzmiv must delete both #[ignore] attributes in crates/oraclemcp-core/tests/c2_stdio_init_token_literal_frame.rs as part of its acceptance.
